PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY JILL H TEITEL
Off Calendar May-13-2026 Continued To Jun-24-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents & Determine Parameters Of Protective Order Regarding Production Of Documents; Declaration Of Michelle M. West, Esq., And Exhibits
Off Calendar Oct-16-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs' motion for a protective order and to compel production of documents is granted.

Under CCP section 2031.060(b), "The court, for good cause shown, may make any order that justice requires to protect any party or other person from unwarranted annoyance, embarrassment, or oppression, or undue burden and expense." The protective order may direct "that the inspection, copying, testing, or sampling be made only on specified terms and conditions" or "[t]hat a trade secret or other confidential research, development, or commercial information not be disclosed, or be disclosed only to specified persons or only in a specified way." (CCP 2031.060(b)(4)-(5).)

Here, Plaintiffs request a sharing provision that would allow discovery in the present case to be shared with attorneys for other plaintiffs prosecuting similar claims against Defendants. Limited sharing among counsel for similarly situated plaintiffs is permitted when it promotes efficiency, and no competitive harm is shown. (Raymond Handling Concepts Corp. v. Superior Court (1995) 39 Cal.App.4th 584, 589.) However, this court finds that Plaintiff's initial proposed language is overly broad and could potentially lead to competitive harm without the sharing provision being conditioned on judicial oversight. The court therefore adopts Plaintiff's revised language (West Decl. 8.), permitting limited sharing if Plaintiffs move the Court for a modification of the protective order. The court declines to include an "Attorney's Eyes Only" tier of confidentiality in the protective order. Defendants have not demonstrated that such a designation is necessary to prevent specific harms that cannot be avoided by less restrictive means. (see Alch v. Superior Court (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1412, 1425.)

The court adopts Defendants' proposed protective order (West Decl. Ex. 2A; Horsey Decl. Ex. 1), with the deletion of any mention of "For Attorney's Eyes Only" confidentiality, and with the addition of Plaintiffs' proposed language ["If Plaintiffs during the pendency of this litigation learn of other pending litigation against the same defendants alleging the same or similar defect allegations, plaintiffs may move the Court for a modification of this Order to consider permitting the sharing of discovery and confidential information which has been provided under terms similar or identical to that of this order."] The parties are ordered to meet and confer to draft a protective order subject to this order. If the parties cannot agree on a protective order after meet and confer efforts, they may move for the court to write a compliant order.

The requests for production of documents at issue appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) Defendants are ordered to produce responsive documents, subject to the protective order, by November 14, 2025.

Proposed Order submitted.

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/SKF)
